title: The ConsortiumMetabolism Class
text: ConsortiumMetabolism (CM) class fundamental building block ramen package. represents single microbial consortium’s metabolic exchange network – directed graph metabolite consumption production member species. CM object stores: Six assay matrices (square, metabolite--metabolite, sparse): Binary, nSpecies, Consumption, Production, EffectiveConsumption, EffectiveProduction Pathway data linking consumed produced metabolites per-pathway summary statistics igraph object directed metabolic network vignette covers construction, accessors, replacement methods, subsetting, inspection CM objects.
code: library(ramen)

title: The ConsortiumMetabolism Class
text: ConsortiumMetabolism() constructor accepts data.frame (tibble) columns species, metabolite, flux. Negative fluxes indicate consumption; positive fluxes indicate production. Custom column names can specified via species_col, metabolite_col, flux_col:
code: edge_data <- data.frame(     species = c(         "Sp_A", "Sp_A", "Sp_A", "Sp_A",         "Sp_B", "Sp_B", "Sp_B",         "Sp_C", "Sp_C", "Sp_C"     ),     met = c(         "met1", "met2", "met3", "met4",         "met1", "met3", "met4",         "met2", "met3", "met4"     ),     flux = c(         -0.30, 0.53, -2.23, 3.31,         2.55, 0.34, -1.85,         -1.30, -0.48, 0.60     ) )  cm_manual <- ConsortiumMetabolism(     edge_data,     name = "manual_example" ) cm_manual #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "manual_example" #> Weighted metabolic network with 4 metabolites. df <- data.frame(     organism = c("Sp_A", "Sp_A", "Sp_B", "Sp_B"),     compound = c("glc", "ac", "glc", "ac"),     rate = c(-1.0, 0.5, 0.8, -0.3) )  cm_custom <- ConsortiumMetabolism(     df,     name = "custom_cols",     species_col = "organism",     metabolite_col = "compound",     flux_col = "rate" ) cm_custom #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "custom_cols" #> Weighted metabolic network with 2 metabolites.

title: The ConsortiumMetabolism Class
text: quick testing demonstration, synCM() generates synthetic consortia random species-metabolite interactions. retrieve raw edge list without constructing CM object, set cm = FALSE:
code: cm_syn <- synCM(     "SyntheticComm",     n_species = 5,     max_met = 8,     seed = 42 ) cm_syn #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "SyntheticComm" #> Weighted metabolic network with 8 metabolites. raw_edges <- synCM(     "raw",     n_species = 3,     max_met = 5,     seed = 1,     cm = FALSE ) head(raw_edges) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   species metabolites fluxes #>   <chr>   <chr>        <dbl> #> 1 ERH100V met2        -4.79  #> 2 ERH100V met1         0.989 #> 3 ERU091V met1         1.73  #> 4 ERU091V met5        -0.916 #> 5 ERU091V met4         4.54  #> 6 ERU091V met2         1.17

title: The ConsortiumMetabolism Class
text: package ships misosoup24, list 56 metabolic solutions MiSoSoup. element tibble columns metabolites, species, fluxes.

title: The ConsortiumMetabolism Class
text: CM object inherits TreeSummarizedExperiment stores six square (metabolite--metabolite) sparse assay matrices. Rows represent consumed metabolites columns represent produced metabolites. matrix encodes different view metabolic network:
code: SummarizedExperiment::assayNames(cm_ms) #> [1] "Binary"               "nSpecies"             "Consumption"          #> [4] "Production"           "EffectiveConsumption" "EffectiveProduction" ## The Binary assay (sparse matrix) SummarizedExperiment::assay(cm_ms, "Binary") #> 14 x 14 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix" #>   [[ suppressing 14 column names 'ac', 'acald', 'ala__D' ... ]] #>                                    #> ac     . 1 . . 1 1 1 . 1 . 1 1 1 1 #> acald  1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . . #> ala__D . 1 . . 1 1 1 . 1 . 1 1 1 1 #> ala__L . 1 . . 1 1 1 . 1 . 1 1 1 1 #> asp__L 1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . . #> co2    . . . . . . . . . . . . . . #> etoh   1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . . #> glu__L . 1 . . 1 1 1 . 1 . 1 1 1 1 #> gly    1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . . #> gthox  . 1 . . 1 1 1 . 1 . 1 1 1 1 #> gthrd  1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . . #> h2o2   1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . . #> h2s    1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . . #> pyr    1 . 1 1 . 1 . 1 . 1 . . . .

text: CM inherits TreeSummarizedExperiment, standard dimension accessors work: rows columns correspond metabolite universe consortium (including synthetic “media” node needed).

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: ramen package provides tools comparing microbial communities based metabolic exchange networks. alignment system quantifies similar two communities functional perspective – species present, metabolite-metabolite pathways catalyse. vignette covers: Pairwise alignment – comparing two communities Multiple alignment – comparing communities set Visualization – heatmaps, networks, score plots Programmatic access – extracting results accessor functions
code: library(ramen)

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: use synCM() generate synthetic communities reproducible random metabolic networks. consortium species random flux values across shared metabolite pool.
code: cm_alpha <- synCM("Alpha", n_species = 5, max_met = 10, seed = 42) cm_beta <- synCM("Beta", n_species = 5, max_met = 10, seed = 43) cm_gamma <- synCM("Gamma", n_species = 4, max_met = 8, seed = 44) cm_delta <- synCM("Delta", n_species = 6, max_met = 10, seed = 45)  cm_alpha #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "Alpha" #> Weighted metabolic network with 10 metabolites.

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: align(CM, CM) compares two ConsortiumMetabolism objects returns ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment (CMA) object.
code: cma <- align(cm_alpha, cm_beta) cma #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment #> Name: NA #> Type: "pairwise" #> Metric: "FOS" #> Score: 0.4474 #> Query: "Alpha", Reference: "Beta"

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Five metrics available via method argument. four individual metrics always computed; method selects becomes primary score.
code: cma_fos <- align(cm_alpha, cm_beta, method = "FOS") cma_jac <- align(cm_alpha, cm_beta, method = "jaccard") cma_bc <- align(cm_alpha, cm_beta, method = "brayCurtis") cma_ro <- align(cm_alpha, cm_beta, method = "redundancyOverlap")  ## All scores are stored regardless of method choice scores(cma_fos) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.4473684 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.265625 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.2616082 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.202381

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Metabolic Alignment Aggregate Score combines four metrics. Weights renormalized metrics unavailable (e.g., unweighted networks).
code: cma_maas <- align(cm_alpha, cm_beta, method = "MAAS") cma_maas@PrimaryScore #> [1] 0.3248702

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: alignment classifies every metabolite-metabolite pathway shared, unique query, unique reference.
code: sp <- sharedPathways(cma) head(sp[, c("consumed", "produced")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   consumed produced #>   <chr>    <chr>    #> 1 met1     met10    #> 2 met3     met10    #> 3 met4     met10    #> 4 met7     met10    #> 5 met8     met10    #> 6 met9     met10  up <- uniquePathways(cma) nrow(sp) # shared #> [1] 17 nrow(up$query) # unique to Alpha #> [1] 26 nrow(up$reference) # unique to Beta #> [1] 21

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Statistical significance assessed degree-preserving network rewiring. query network’s pathways shuffled preserving metabolite’s degree, metric recomputed null.
code: cma_p <- align(   cm_alpha,   cm_beta,   method = "FOS",   computePvalue = TRUE,   nPermutations = 99L ) cma_p@Pvalue #> [1] 0.75

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: align(CMS) computes pairwise similarities across consortia ConsortiumMetabolismSet returns CMA Type = "multiple".
code: cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(   list(cm_alpha, cm_beta, cm_gamma, cm_delta),   name = "Demo" ) cma_mult <- align(cms) cma_mult

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: SimilarityMatrix n x n symmetric matrix 1s diagonal. FOS, derived directly pre-computed CMS overlap matrix, guaranteeing numerical consistency.
code: round(similarityMatrix(cma_mult), 3) #>       Alpha  Beta Gamma Delta #> Alpha 1.000 0.447 0.556 0.538 #> Beta  0.447 1.000 0.481 0.526 #> Gamma 0.556 0.481 1.000 0.481 #> Delta 0.538 0.526 0.481 1.000

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: PrimaryScore multiple alignment median pairwise scores. Full summary statistics available via scores().

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Pathway prevalence counts many consortia share metabolite-metabolite pathway. enables classification pathways core (present consortia) niche (present ).
code: prev <- prevalence(cma_mult) head(prev[order(-prev$nConsortia), ]) #>    consumed produced nConsortia proportion #> 16     met1     met2          4       1.00 #> 22     met7     met2          4       1.00 #> 41     met1     met5          4       1.00 #> 66     met1     met8          4       1.00 #> 8      met1    met10          3       0.75 #> 9      met3    met10          3       0.75  ## Distribution table(prev$nConsortia) #>  #>  1  2  3  4  #> 32 24 17  4

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: heatmap shows pairwise similarities dendrogram-based ordering. Similarity heatmap across four consortia.
code: plot(cma_mult, type = "heatmap")

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: network view shows shared (green), query-unique (blue), reference-unique (red) pathways directed metabolite flow graph. Pathway network: Alpha vs Beta.
code: plot(cma, type = "network")

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: scores bar chart works pairwise multiple alignments. Pairwise metric scores. Multiple alignment summary scores.

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: ConsortiumMetabolismSet (CMS) class groups multiple ConsortiumMetabolism objects single analysis unit. CMS constructed, ramen automatically: Expands binary matrices shared universal metabolite space Computes pairwise overlap scores (Functional Overlap Score) every pair consortia Builds dendrogram via hierarchical clustering dissimilarity matrix (1 - overlap) vignette covers CMS construction, accessors, overlap matrix, dendrogram operations, species pathway classification, cluster extraction.
code: library(ramen)

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: ConsortiumMetabolismSet() constructor accepts number ConsortiumMetabolism objects (list ), plus name. list CMs works equally well:
code: cm_alpha <- synCM("Alpha", n_species = 5, max_met = 10, seed = 42) cm_beta <- synCM("Beta", n_species = 5, max_met = 10, seed = 43) cm_gamma <- synCM("Gamma", n_species = 4, max_met = 8, seed = 44) cm_delta <- synCM("Delta", n_species = 6, max_met = 10, seed = 45)  cms_syn <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(     cm_alpha, cm_beta, cm_gamma, cm_delta,     name = "SyntheticSet" ) cms_syn cm_list <- list(cm_alpha, cm_beta) cms_small <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(     cm_list,     name = "SmallSet" ) cms_small

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: misosoup24 dataset contains 56 metabolic solutions. construct CMs first four combine CMS.
code: data("misosoup24")  ## Build 4 CMs from the dataset cm_ms <- lapply(seq_len(4), function(i) {     ConsortiumMetabolism(         misosoup24[[i]],         name = names(misosoup24)[i],         species_col = "species",         metabolite_col = "metabolites",         flux_col = "fluxes"     ) })  cms_ms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(     cm_ms,     name = "MiSoSoup_subset" ) cms_ms

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: Printing CMS displays name, number consortia, description.
code: cms_syn #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismSet #> Name: "SyntheticSet" #> Containing 4 consortia. #> Description: NA

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
code: name(cms_syn) #> [1] "SyntheticSet" description(cms_syn) #> [1] NA

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: CMS, species() returns tibble species across consortia number distinct pathways species catalyses, sorted descending order.

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: Returns sorted character vector metabolites across universal metabolite space.
code: metabolites(cms_syn) #>  [1] "met1"  "met10" "met2"  "met3"  "met4"  "met5"  "met6"  "met7"  "met8"  #> [10] "met9"

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: Returns pathway information across consortia. type argument filters specific subsets.
code: ## All pathways with consortium counts pw <- pathways(cms_syn) head(pw) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   consumed produced n_species n_cons #>   <chr>    <chr>        <int>  <int> #> 1 met7     met2             5      4 #> 2 met1     met5             4      4 #> 3 met1     met2             4      4 #> 4 met1     met8             6      4 #> 5 met8     met4             3      3 #> 6 met8     met5             4      3

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: overlap matrix stores pairwise dissimilarity scores (1 - Functional Overlap Score) consortia. FOS Szymkiewicz-Simpson coefficient applied binary metabolite-metabolite matrices. Values near 0 diagonal indicate self-identity; -diagonal values close 0 mean high similarity.

title: The ConsortiumMetabolismSet Class
text: dendrogram built overlap (dissimilarity) matrix using hierarchical clustering stored Dendrogram slot.  NodeData slot contains positions internal dendrogram nodes, used extractCluster() plot().
code: dend <- cms_syn@Dendrogram[[1]] plot(dend, main = "Consortium dendrogram") cms_syn@NodeData #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>       x     y original_node_id node_id #>   <dbl> <dbl>            <int>   <int> #> 1   2.5 0.785                1       1 #> 2   3.5 0.676                5       2 #> 3   1.5 0.632                2       3

title: Visualisation
text: ramen provides plot() methods three core classes: ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment. vignette demonstrates plot type synthetic example data.
code: library(ramen)

title: Visualisation
text: create four synthetic consortia, combine CMS, compute pairwise multiple alignments.
code: cm_alpha <- synCM("Alpha", n_species = 5, max_met = 10, seed = 42) cm_beta <- synCM("Beta", n_species = 5, max_met = 10, seed = 43) cm_gamma <- synCM("Gamma", n_species = 4, max_met = 8, seed = 44) cm_delta <- synCM("Delta", n_species = 6, max_met = 10, seed = 45)  cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(     cm_alpha, cm_beta, cm_gamma, cm_delta,     name = "Demo" )  cma_pair <- align(cm_alpha, cm_beta) cma_mult <- align(cms)

title: Visualisation
text: plot(CM) renders directed metabolic flow network using igraph. Nodes coloured role: lightblue = source (outgoing), salmon = sink (incoming), yellow = intermediate (). type argument selects assay matrix use edge weights.

title: Visualisation
text: default view shows structure metabolic network edge present weight 1. Binary metabolic network Alpha.
code: plot(cm_alpha, type = "Binary")

title: Visualisation
text: Edge weights reflect many species catalyse pathway. nSpecies network Alpha.
code: plot(cm_alpha, type = "nSpecies")

title: Visualisation
text: Consumption production views weight edges summed flux magnitudes. Consumption-weighted network. Production-weighted network.
code: plot(cm_alpha, type = "Consumption") plot(cm_alpha, type = "Production")

title: Visualisation
text: effective diversity measures indicate evenly species contribute pathway (Shannon-based effective number species). Effective consumption network. Effective production network.
code: plot(cm_alpha, type = "EffectiveConsumption") plot(cm_alpha, type = "EffectiveProduction")

title: Visualisation
text: plot(CMS) draws hierarchical clustering dendrogram numbered internal nodes. node IDs used extractCluster() pull sub-clusters. CMS dendrogram numbered nodes.
code: plot(cms)

title: Visualisation
text: can supply tibble mapping leaf labels colours. CMS dendrogram coloured labels.
code: colour_map <- data.frame(     label = c("Alpha", "Beta", "Gamma", "Delta"),     colour = c("steelblue", "firebrick",                "forestgreen", "darkorange") ) plot(cms, label_colours = colour_map)

title: Visualisation
text: CMA plot() method supports three types: "heatmap", "network", "scores". default depends alignment type: "network" pairwise, "heatmap" multiple.

title: Visualisation
text: heatmap shows pairwise FOS similarities dendrogram-based ordering. Values range 0 (overlap) 1 (identical). Similarity heatmap.
code: plot(cma_mult, type = "heatmap")

title: Visualisation
text: network view visualises alignment result directed metabolite flow graph. Edge colours indicate source: green = shared pathways, blue = unique query (Alpha), red = unique reference (Beta). Pairwise alignment network.
code: plot(cma_pair, type = "network")

title: Visualisation
text: score bar chart displays computed similarity metrics pairwise alignment. Pairwise alignment scores.
code: plot(cma_pair, type = "scores")

title: Visualisation
text: multiple alignment, scores plot shows summary statistics across pairwise comparisons. Multiple alignment scores.
code: plot(cma_mult, type = "scores") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`). #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_text()`).

title: Visualisation
text: fine-grained control network layout, can call plotDirectedFlow() directly igraph object. function underlying CM CMA network plots. Custom directed flow plot.
code: g <- igraph::graph_from_adjacency_matrix(     SummarizedExperiment::assay(cm_beta, "nSpecies"),     mode = "directed",     weighted = TRUE )  plotDirectedFlow(     g,     color_edges_by_weight = TRUE,     edge_width_range = c(0.5, 2),     vertex_size = 12,     vertex_label_cex = 0.7,     main = "Beta - nSpecies (custom)" )

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
text: Creates ConsortiumMetabolism object representing metabolic interactions microbial community. object stores metabolite consumption production different species, along flux sums effective fluxes.

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
text: data data.frame columns species, metabolites fluxes. Fluxes can weighted unweighted (magnitude 1). name Character scalar giving consortium name. species_col Character scalar species column name, defaults "species". metabolite_col Character scalar metabolite column name, defaults "met". flux_col Character scalar flux column name, defaults "flux". ... Additional arguments passed constructor.

text: ConsortiumMetabolism object.

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
text: Name character. Display name consortium. Description character. Optional short description. Pathways data.frame. Pathway list metabolic interactions per-pathway metrics (species, flux sums, effective diversity). Weighted logical. Whether flux magnitudes used. InputData data.frame. Original input data (species, metabolite, flux columns). Metabolites character. Unique metabolite identifiers. Graphs list. List containing igraph object metabolic network.

title: Constructor for ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment Objects — ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment-class
text: Name character. Display name alignment. Description character. Optional short description. Type character. One "pairwise", "multiple", "search". Metric character. Similarity metric used (e.g., "FOS"). Params list. Additional parameters passed alignment method. QueryName character. Name query consortium (pairwise). ReferenceName character. Name reference consortium (pairwise). Scores list. Named list computed score components. PrimaryScore numeric. Primary similarity score, 0 1. Pvalue numeric. Permutation p-value, 0 1. SharedPathways data.frame. Pathways present query reference. UniqueQuery data.frame. Pathways unique query. UniqueReference data.frame. Pathways unique reference. SimilarityMatrix matrix. Pairwise similarity matrix (multiple alignment). ConsensusPathways data.frame. Consensus network pathways (multiple alignment). Prevalence data.frame. Pathway prevalence across consortia (multiple alignment). Dendrogram list. Hierarchical clustering dendrogram (multiple alignment). Pathways data.frame. Combined pathway list. Graphs list. List igraph objects. Metabolites data.frame. Metabolite mapping table.

title: Align Consortium Metabolisms — align
text: Computes functional alignment consortium metabolisms. Dispatches combination x y: align(CM, CM): Pairwise alignment two consortia align(CMS): Multiple alignment across consortia set align(CM, CMS): Database search – align one consortium set

title: Align Consortium Metabolisms — align
code: align(x, y, method = "FOS", ...)  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolism,ConsortiumMetabolism' align(x, y, method = "FOS", computePvalue = FALSE, nPermutations = 999L, ...)  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolismSet,missing' align(x, y, method = "FOS", BPPARAM = BiocParallel::SerialParam(), ...)  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolism,ConsortiumMetabolismSet' align(x, y, method = "FOS", BPPARAM = BiocParallel::SerialParam(), ...)

title: Align Consortium Metabolisms — align
text: x ConsortiumMetabolism object (query). y ConsortiumMetabolismSet object (database). method Character scalar specifying metric. ... Additional arguments (currently unused). computePvalue Logical; compute permutation p-value? Default FALSE. nPermutations Integer; number permutations. Default 999L. BPPARAM BiocParallel::BiocParallelParam object. Default BiocParallel::SerialParam().

title: Align Consortium Metabolisms — align
text: ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object. ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object type "pairwise". ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object type "multiple". ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object type "search".

title: Align Consortium Metabolisms — align

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways
text: Retrieves pathways representing metabolic interactions species. default, returns concise summary columns consumed, produced, n_species. ConsortiumMetabolismSet objects, n_cons included well. Set verbose = TRUE return full pathway data including flux statistics, indices, per-species detail. argument type can used return specific types pathways ConsortiumMetabolismSet object: "" returns pathways "pan-cons" returns pathways present consortia "niche" returns niche pathways specific consortia "core" returns core metabolic pathways shared across species "aux" returns auxiliary pathways found species

code: pathways(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolism' pathways(object, verbose = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolismSet' pathways(   object,   type = c("all", "pan-cons", "niche", "core", "aux"),   quantileCutoff = 0.1,   verbose = FALSE )  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment' pathways(object)

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways
text: object ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object. ... Object specific arguments. See methods details. verbose Logical scalar. FALSE (default), returns concise summary columns consumed, produced, n_species. TRUE, returns full pathway data including flux statistics, effective values, per-species detail. type Character scalar giving type pathways output. quantileCutoff Numeric scalar 0 1 giving quantile threshold use filtering pathways. "pan-cons" "core" types, pathways 1 - quantileCutoff returned. "niche" "aux" types, pathways quantileCutoff returned. Defaults 0.1 (.e., top/bottom 10 percent).

Defaults 10. vertex_label_cex Numeric scalar. character expansion factor vertex labels. Defaults 0.8. edge_arrow_size Numeric scalar. size arrows edges. title: ramen 0.0.0.9001
text: Initial development release. Three core S4 classes: ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment, inheriting TreeSummarizedExperiment. Import microbial metabolic network data MiSoSoup YAML format via importMisosoup(). Pairwise multiple alignment consortium metabolisms using functional overlap scores (FOS), Jaccard, Bray-Curtis, redundancy overlap metrics. Visualization methods including heatmaps, network plots, alignment score plots. Functional group analysis hierarchical clustering species shared metabolic pathways.
